assertions.says: A callout with a basename that matches exactly one touched module gets upgraded to the full path
The resolved path matches the module from modules_touched, not a fabricated path
A basename matching two or more touched modules stays as a basename
A basename matching zero touched modules stays unchanged
A callout file that already contains a path separator is not re-resolved
Build concern file fields are resolved using the same logic as callout files
Existing proof chain entries get their callout files resolved when the chain is rewritten
Path boundary checking prevents a file named route.ts from matching subroute.ts
Querying a file returns all callouts associated with that file
Each returned callout includes its category, summary, and originating feature name
The --json flag produces valid parseable JSON output
JSON output includes callouts array and build_concerns array per result
Querying a file with no proof data shows a clean message instead of an error
The no-data message names the queried file so the user knows what was searched
Querying when no proof_chain.json exists shows a clean message, not an error
The query function lives in proofSummary.ts and has no CLI imports
A full-path query matches a full-path callout exactly
A basename query matches a full-path callout via path suffix
A full-path query matches a legacy basename callout
A query for route.ts does not match a callout filed under subroute.ts
The result includes how many pipeline cycles touched the queried file
The result includes the date of the most recent pipeline cycle for the file
Callouts with null file fields are never returned by any file query
Querying multiple files returns separate results for each file
